source.title: Creation of Novel Protein Variants with CRISPR/Cas9-Mediated Mutagenesis: Turning a Screening By-Product into a Discovery Tool

initial_request.research_problem: Deciphering functional consequences of DNA variation by creating novel protein variants using CRISPR/Cas9-mediated mutagenesis, turning a screening by-product into a discovery tool.
initial_request.known_context: Previous genome-wide loss-of-function screens in A375 melanoma cells identified sgRNAs that sensitize to MAPK inhibitors. Unexpectedly, an sgRNA targeting MAP2K1 K59 conferred strong drug resistance.
constraints: Pooled screening with limited library size
input_data: sgRNA sequences targeting MAP2K1 and BRAF

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Prior genome-wide loss-of-function screens in A375 cells with vemurafenib and selumetinib expected to identify sgRNAs that sensitize cells to drugs.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Unexpected enrichment of an sgRNA targeting MAP2K1 K59 contradicted expected loss-of-function.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: NHEJ-mediated repair of the sgRNA cut site creates drug-resistant MEK1 variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Investigate the mechanism behind the unexpected enrichment.
evolution_trajectory.observation: An sgRNA targeting MAP2K1 K59 generated the strongest drug-resistance phenotype for both vemurafenib and selumetinib.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The unexpected result led to the hypothesis that NHEJ creates drug-resistant variants, motivating deep sequencing to characterize allelic diversity.

evidence.quote_or_span: an sgRNA targeting the gene MAP2K1 (which encodes the protein MEK1) at the site encoding K59 generated the strongest drug-resistance phenotype for both vemurafenib and selumetinib
We hypothesized that this unexpected result was the consequence of NHEJ-mediated repair of the sgRNA cut site, which led to the creation of drug resistant variants of MEK1.

To validate the activity of the variants produced by the K59 sgRNA, we synthesized ORFs with these deletions, introduced them into A375 cells via lentivirus, and treated with selumetinib and vemurafenib.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Hypothesis that NHEJ creates drug-resistant MEK1 variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to characterize the allelic diversity generated by the K59 sgRNA.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Deep sequencing will reveal extensive allelic diversity at the target site.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform deep sequencing of the MAP2K1 K59 sgRNA target site in unselected cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 1,669 variants present at ≥25 RPM, encoding 1,351 unique amino acid sequences.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The extensive allelic diversity allowed comparison of allele abundance between drug-treated and unselected cells to identify enriched variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: In contrast, for cells treated with the MAP2K1 sgRNA in the absence of selection, only 38% of the reads aligned to wild-type, and there were 1,669 variants present at 25 RPM or greater, coding for 1,351 unique MAP2K1 amino acid sequences (Fig 1B).

To validate the activity of the variants produced by the K59 sgRNA, we synthesized ORFs with these deletions, introduced them into A375 cells via lentivirus, and treated with selumetinib and vemurafenib.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Deep sequencing revealed 1,669 variants in unselected cells.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which variants are enriched under drug selection?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Drug-resistant variants will be enriched in vemurafenib-treated cells.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare allele abundance between vemurafenib-treated and unselected cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Greater than 100-fold enrichment for multiple variants.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Enriched variants were analyzed for common features, revealing five amino acid deletions in an alpha-helix.

evidence.quote_or_span: We next compared allele abundance between vemurafenib-treated and unselected cells, and observed greater than 100-fold enrichment for multiple variants
We next compared allele abundance between vemurafenib-treated and unselected cells, and observed greater than 100-fold enrichment for multiple variants (Fig 1C).

In contrast, for cells treated with the MAP2K1 sgRNA in the absence of selection, only 38% of the reads aligned to wild-type, and there were 1,669 variants present at 25 RPM or greater, coding for 1,351 unique MAP2K1 amino acid sequences (Fig 1B).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Multiple variants showed >100-fold enrichment under vemurafenib.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What structural features do enriched variants share?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Enriched variants contain deletions in a regulatory alpha-helix.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze the sequence of enriched variants.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Many strongly enriched variants contained five amino acid deletions in an alpha-helix that limits MEK1 activity.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The identified deletions were functionally validated by ORF overexpression.

evidence.quote_or_span: Many of the variants strongly enriched in vemurafenib contained five amino acid deletions. The amino acids in this region form an alpha-helix that functions to limit the phosphotransferase activity of MEK1
We next compared allele abundance between vemurafenib-treated and unselected cells, and observed greater than 100-fold enrichment for multiple variants

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Five amino acid deletions in alpha-helix were identified in enriched variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do these deletions directly confer drug resistance?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: ORF overexpression of deletion variants will confer drug resistance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Synthesize ORFs with deletions, introduce into A375 cells, and test drug sensitivity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Overexpression of deletion variants conferred drug resistance comparable to MEK-DD, while wild-type MEK1 did not.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validation of the single sgRNA approach motivated expansion to a tiled library design for broader variant discovery.

evidence.quote_or_span: To validate the activity of the variants produced by the K59 sgRNA, we synthesized ORFs with these deletions, introduced them into A375 cells via lentivirus, and treated with selumetinib and vemurafenib.
variant [9] and the Q56P variant, whereas overexpression of wild-type MEK1 did not produce appreciable resistance (Fig 2).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Single sgRNA approach validated for generating drug-resistant variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can the approach be scaled to generate variants across entire genes?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A tiled sgRNA library targeting all coding exons will enable variant discovery across MAP2K1 and BRAF.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a tiled sgRNA library targeting MAP2K1 and BRAF.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Library designed with 217 sgRNAs for MAP2K1, 279 for BRAF, and 100 non-targeting controls.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The library was used for pooled screening in two cell lines with drug selection.

evidence.quote_or_span: We next expanded this technique to generate variants across the length of a gene by creating a tiling pool of all possible sgRNAs targeting MAP2K1 (n = 217) and BRAF (n = 279), as well as 100 non-targeting control sgRNAs.

evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which sgRNAs generate drug-resistant variants in different cellular contexts?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Pooled screening will identify sgRNAs enriched under drug selection.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen the library in A375 and MEL-JUSO cells with vemurafenib and selumetinib.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Screening performed in three biological replicates per cell line. Some populations fell below the 500 cells/sgRNA bottleneck due to strong positive selection.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Despite bottlenecking, sgRNA enrichment patterns revealed drug-specific resistance, prompting analysis of differential resistance.

evidence.quote_or_span: The sgRNA MAP2K1/BRAF mini-library was screened in three biological replicates in each of two cell lines.
Vemurafenib (PLX-4032, Selleckchem S1267) was screened at 2 $\mu$ M, and selumetinib (AZD-6244, Selleckchem S1008) was screened at 1.5 $\mu$ M.

Cells were selected for 5 days with puromycin (1 $\mu$ g/mL for A375 and 2 $\mu$ g/mL for MEL-JUSO) to remove uninfected cells, then each population of transduced cells were split to vemurafenib (2 $\mu$ M), selumetinib (1.5 $\mu$ M), and unselected arms.

Due to the strong positive selection that occurs in the presence of the small molecules, however, some populations fell below this bottleneck; in this case all surviving cells were reseeded at each passage.
Throughout the screen, cells were split at a density to maintain a representation of $\sim$ 500 cells per sgRNA.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Pooled screening completed with some bottlenecking.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Some sgRNAs will confer resistance only to selumetinib by disrupting drug binding.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare sgRNA enrichment between vemurafenib and selumetinib screens.
evolution_trajectory.observation: sgRNAs targeting V211, S212, and I111 enriched only in selumetinib, suggesting disruption of drug binding rather than constitutive activation.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Drug-specific resistance patterns prompted comparison across cell lines to assess context dependence.

evidence.quote_or_span: We hypothesized that sgRNAs that led to only selumetinib resistance were disrupting selumetinib binding to MEK1, rather than producing constitutively active mutants. Consistent with this notion, two selumetinib-specific sgRNAs target sites that encode V211 and S212, both of which are located in the binding pocket for this class of allosteric inhibitors

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Drug-specific sgRNA enrichment observed in A375 cells.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do resistance patterns differ between cell lines?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Cellular context will influence which sgRNAs confer resistance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare selumetinib-resistant sgRNAs between A375 and MEL-JUSO cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: In MEL-JUSO, only I111- and K59-targeting sgRNAs enriched; many sgRNAs that scored in A375 failed in MEL-JUSO.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The failure of many MAP2K1 sgRNAs in MEL-JUSO led to screening for BRAF variants in that cell line.

evidence.quote_or_span: For MAP2K1, I111- and K59-targeting sgRNAs were enriched, but many other sgRNAs that scored in A375 cells failed to provide significant enrichment in MEL-JUSO cells
For MAP2K1, I111- and K59-targeting sgRNAs were enriched, but many other sgRNAs that scored in A375 cells failed to provide significant enrichment in MEL-JUSO cells (Fig 3D).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MAP2K1 sgRNAs showed limited enrichment in MEL-JUSO.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Can BRAF variants be identified in MEL-JUSO?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Screening the tiling library in MEL-JUSO will identify BRAF sgRNAs that confer selumetinib resistance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen the library for selumetinib resistance in MEL-JUSO cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: One sgRNA targeting BRAF Q494 enriched in MEL-JUSO cells.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The enriched BRAF Q494 sgRNA was characterized by deep sequencing to identify specific variants.

evidence.quote_or_span: one sgRNA targeting Q494 enriched in MEL-JUSO cells

evolution_trajectory.state_before: BRAF Q494 sgRNA enriched in MEL-JUSO selumetinib screen.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What variants are generated by the Q494 sgRNA?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Deep sequencing will reveal a dominant deletion variant.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Deep sequence the BRAF Q494 target site in unselected and selumetinib-treated MEL-JUSO cells.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 1,353 unique variants at ≥25 RPM in unselected cells. The most-abundant allele in selumetinib-treated cells was a 1-amino acid deletion of Q494 (65% of reads, 3.2-fold enrichment).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The identification of BRAF variants in MEL-JUSO, contrasting with MAP2K1 variants in A375, highlighted the impact of cellular context.

evidence.quote_or_span: In the absence of selection, 1,353 unique variants were present at 25 reads per million or greater
The most-abundant allele in selumetinib-treated cells, accounting for 65% of all sequencing reads, contained a 1 amino acid deletion of Q494
In the absence of selection, 1,353 unique variants were present at 25 reads per million or greater (S5 Table).

The most-abundant allele in selumetinib-treated cells, accounting for 65% of all sequencing reads, contained a 1 amino acid deletion of Q494, which resides in alpha-helix C, although the overall enrichment for this allele was modest (3.2 fold) as it was already highly abundant in the unselected population (Fig 4B).
these distinct contexts facilitated the identification of MAP2K1 variants in A375 cells and BRAF variants in MEL-JUSO cells
These results emphasize the impact of cellular context on variant characterization; both A375 and MEL-JUSO cells harbor activating mutations in the RAS/RAF/MEK signaling cascade.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MAP2K1 variants identified in A375, BRAF variants in MEL-JUSO.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Why do different cell lines yield different variant profiles?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Cellular context, including background mutations, influences which variants are identified.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Interpret results in light of cell line genotypes.
evolution_trajectory.observation: A375 (BRAF V600E) facilitated MAP2K1 variant identification; MEL-JUSO (NRAS Q61L) facilitated BRAF variant identification. Cellular context impacts variant discovery.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: This limitation concludes the trajectory, emphasizing the need for multiple cellular contexts in variant discovery.

evidence.quote_or_span: these distinct contexts facilitated the identification of MAP2K1 variants in A375 cells and BRAF variants in MEL-JUSO cells
However, these distinct contexts facilitated the identification of MAP2K1 variants in A375 cells and BRAF variants in MEL-JUSO cells.
These results emphasize the impact of cellular context on variant characterization; both A375 and MEL-JUSO cells harbor activating mutations in the RAS/RAF/MEK signaling cascade.

We speculate that A375 cells, with the BRAF V600E mutation, are more reliant on MAPK signaling and are thus a more sensitive model system in which to find MAP2K1 mutations, whereas the NRAS Q61L mutation activates additional survival pathways in MEL-JUSO cells.

success_verification.final_conclusion: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated NHEJ mutagenesis can generate diverse protein variants, enabling discovery of drug-resistant alleles. Cellular context significantly influences which variants are identified.
limitations: Cellular context impacts variant identification (A375 vs. MEL-JUSO)
Bottlenecking due to strong positive selection
Modest enrichment of some variants (e.g., BRAF Q494 deletion 3.2-fold)
